title: Surgical redline of a vendor-favoured SaaS MSA
note: Does the agent read the MSA, then apply_redline with surgical, balanced edits?
prompt: You are our in-house commercial counsel and we are the CUSTOMER. Review the attached vendor SaaS agreement "SecureScan-MSA.docx" and redline it to protect us — apply your changes as tracked changes using apply_redline. Make surgical, balanced edits (carve-outs, deemed-direct, super-caps where appropriate); do not rip-and-replace whole clauses. Work through EVERY one-sided clause: the liability cap, the one-sided indemnity, the IP assignment, the Customer Data licence, the warranty disclaimer, the unilateral fee increase and the auto-renewal/no-termination term. Read the whole agreement first, then apply ALL your edits in a SINGLE apply_redline call (it takes a batch of many edits and re-redlines the original on each call, so one call must cover everything).
final_answer_excerpt:
All 10 tracked changes applied to **SecureScan-MSA (redlined).docx**. Here is a summary of every edit and the problem it fixes:

| # | Clause | Problem | What we changed |
|---|--------|---------|-----------------|
| **1** | §2 Services | Vendor can eliminate any feature at any time, no consent needed | Permits non-material changes without consent; **material reductions** in core functionality require our prior written consent |
| **2** | §3 Fees | Vendor can raise fees unlimitedly on 30 days' notice | Caps increases at **5% per 12-month period** with **60 days' notice** |
| **3** | §4 Term | No termination-for-cause right — locked in for 3 years even if Vendor breaches | Adds **30-day cure / termination for material breach** by either party |
| **4** | §5 IP (first sentence) | All custom …
judge_verdict:
**Verdict: STRONG**

- **Surgical edits** – targeted inserts (e.g., “non-material,” fee cap, mutual termination, IP carve-out, data license scope, warranty language) rather than full rewrites; preserves original structure.
- **Proper balance mechanisms** – uses carve-outs from cap (confidentiality, indemnity, IP infringement, gross negligence), mutual IP indemnity, and customer-specific IP retention, not just numerical bumps.
- **Coherent across agreement** – data license restriction aligns with Section 5; warranty of non-infringement supports mutual indemnity; cap carve-outs track indemnity/confidentiality obligations.
- **Not over-protective** – leaves vendor-favoured term length, non-refundable fees, no termination for convenience, “as is” for non-warranty items, and Delaware exclusive jurisdiction.
- **Not under-protective** – fixes the critical one-sidedness: unlimited data use, unilateral service changes, no vendor warranties, tiny 1-month cap, missing vendor indemnity.
